action.reasoning: Observation: ensemble_search failed because current model.py/predict.py still instantiate only one TrafficModel variant, while existing checkpoints include at least two incompatible architectures; the workspace also shows truncated files, so the safest path is to rewrite model.py, predict.py, and ensemble_search.py fully. Analysis: we already have a strong public checkpoint (ema_weighted.pt, mae_60=3.5452). The highest expected-value move is a no-training iteration: make loading architecture-aware, evaluate all saved checkpoints and simple blends on validation, and if a blend beats ema_weighted on val_60, submit the ensemble metadata JSON as the checkpoint because predict.py already supports JSON ensembles. This is low risk, fast, and exploits existing assets. Decision: fully rewrite model.py with V0/V1 + checkpoint-driven builder, rewrite predict.py to use it for both .pt and .json checkpoints, rewrite ensemble_search.py to score singles/pairs/triples robustly, run local tests on both single and ensemble checkpoints, then submit the best ensemble JSON if validation improves; otherwise submit ema_weighted.pt again only if needed.
action.command: python ensemble_search.py && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ema_weighted.pt --output local_test/output_single.json && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_meta.json --output local_test/output_ensemble.json
